explanations.8: nohup runs the Chrome command in the background so the script doesn't wait for it to finish, and the backslash at the end continues the command on the next line.
explanations.10: The > /dev/null 2>&1 & redirects all Chrome output to nowhere and the & at the end puts it in the background so the script can continue.
explanations.13: $! is a special variable that holds the process ID (PID) of the last command that ran in the background.
explanations.15: This tells the shell checker to ignore certain warnings about variable expansion in the next line.
explanations.16: The -d memory_limit=-1 flag tells PHP to use unlimited memory instead of the default limit.
